title: Writing data guides
text: Every spreadsheet template accompanied data guide — YAML file documents data located within template interpreted. guide serves blueprint tells excelDataGuide package exactly find read data. data guide human-editable, computer-readable YAML file. YAML syntax simple: use colons (:) key-value pairs, hyphens (-) lists, indentation nesting. section walks constructing guide step step.

title: Writing data guides
text: Every guide begins five pieces metadata describe template : guide.version (required): version guide (template). Use major.minor format, e.g., '1.0' '2.1'. allows track changes guide independently template. template.name (required): short, descriptive name template (e.g., 'myassay', 'fluorescence_assay'). used identify template guide belongs checked runtime enable template name validation. template.min.version (required): minimum template version guide compatible. Use major.minor format (e.g., '1.0'). template.max.version (required, can null): maximum template version guide compatible. upper limit, use ~ (YAML null) indicate “newer version OK”. example, template.max.version: ~ means guide works template versions template.min.version onwards. plate.format (conditionally required): microplate format used experiments. Valid values 24, 48, 96, 384 (referring number wells). required guide includes platedata locations (see ). guide uses validate platedata ranges correct dimensions.
code: guide.version: '1.0' template.name: myassay template.min.version: '1.0' template.max.version: '2.0' plate.format: 96

what: Step 2: Add your first location — template version cell
title: Writing data guides
text: .template location special, required location records template version single cell. allows package check runtime data file created compatible template version. : - Reads cell B2 sheet named description - Stores value variable called version within .template group - translate: false means translation applied (cell value used -) location must present every guide. package uses version value check compatibility template.min.version template.max.version.
code: locations:   - sheet: description     type: cells     varname: .template     translate: false     variables:       - name: version         cell: B2

what: Step 3: Add metadata as key-value pairs
title: Writing data guides
text: Metadata experimenter names, dates, study IDs typically stored key-value pairs template. One location entry can index multiple ranges. : - Reads two ranges (A5:B8 A10:B12) description sheet - left column range contains keys (variable names); right column contains values - translate: true means keys translated long names (spreadsheet) short names (used script) using translations section defined later - atomicclass list data types, one per key-value pair order. example, first three key-value pairs coerced character, character, date respectively, numeric additional pairs (coercion cycles list needed). values coerced character default atomicclass omitted. Combining multiple ranges one variable: Multiple ranges combined single named list (metadata example). convenient metadata scattered across multiple sheets ranges logically belongs together.
code: - sheet: description     type: keyvalue     varname: metadata     translate: true     atomicclass:       - character       - character       - date       - numeric     ranges:       - A5:B8       - A10:B12

title: Writing data guides
text: Parameters constant across experiments (e.g., acceptance criteria, standard concentrations defined SOPs) best stored dedicated _parameters sheet. indexed like metadata: , values coerced numeric. Use translate: false parameter names already format want script.
code: - sheet: _parameters     type: keyvalue     varname: parameters     translate: false     atomicclass: numeric     ranges:       - A2:B5       - A8:B10

title: Writing data guides
text: template records plate data (e.g., fluorescence measurements 96-well plate), use platedata type: : - Reads data plate format (e.g., well positions measurements) - range block data row-column layout physical plate - Multiple ranges stacked vertically single data frame - resulting data frame includes columns row col (well positions) plus one column per measured variable, coerced corresponding atomicclass example, first three variables character, numeric, numeric respectively. first range (A1:M9) represents rows –H plate (8 rows) 12 columns data.
code: - sheet: _data     type: platedata     varname: plate     translate: false     atomicclass:       - character       - numeric       - numeric     ranges:       - A1:M9       - A11:M19

title: Writing data guides
text: Tables column headers multiple rows data indexed using table type: : - Reads table range C3:E10 - first row contains column names (variable names) - values coerced specified atomicclass - result data frame, list
code: - sheet: results     type: table     varname: userresults     translate: false     atomicclass: numeric     ranges:       - C3:E10

title: Writing data guides
text: Sometimes need read isolated values single cells give names guide (rather relying names spreadsheet): : - Reads individual cells assigns names specified guide - spread_well_1 gets value cell G6, spread_well_2 G33 - values coerced numeric - result named list useful values scattered spreadsheet’s cell-naming scheme doesn’t align script’s variable-naming scheme.
code: - sheet: analysis     type: cells     varname: qc_checks     translate: false     atomicclass: numeric     variables:       - name: spread_well_1         cell: G6       - name: spread_well_2         cell: G33

title: Writing data guides
text: spreadsheet uses long, user-friendly variable names (e.g., “Date experiment”) script prefers short, code-friendly names (e.g., date), define translations section: use translations: - Set translate: true location apply translations variable names - Translations optional; don’t define , set translate: false locations - package provides long_to_shortnames() short_to_longnames() functions convert formats
code: translations:   - long: "Date of experiment"     short: date   - long: "Experimenter name"     short: experimenter   - long: "Study identifier"     short: studyID   - long: "Plate identifier"     short: plateID

title: Writing data guides
text: complete, working guide small fluorescence assay template:
code: guide.version: '1.0' template.name: simple_fluorescence template.min.version: '1.0' template.max.version: ~ plate.format: 96  locations:   # Required: template version   - sheet: description     type: cells     varname: .template     translate: false     variables:       - name: version         cell: B2    # Metadata: user and experiment information   - sheet: description     type: keyvalue     varname: metadata     translate: true     atomicclass:       - character       - character       - date       - numeric     ranges:       - A5:B8    # Parameters: SOP-defined constants   - sheet: _parameters     type: keyvalue     varname: parameters     translate: false     atomicclass: numeric     ranges:       - A2:B5    # Plate data: fluorescence measurements   - sheet: _data     type: platedata     varname: fluorescence     translate: false     atomicclass: numeric     ranges:       - A1:M9       - A11:M19    # Quality control: derived metrics from analysis sheet   - sheet: analysis     type: cells     varname: qc     translate: false     atomicclass: numeric     variables:       - name: plate_uniformity         cell: D3       - name: signal_range         cell: D4  translations:   - long: "Experimenter name"     short: experimenter   - long: "Date of experiment"     short: date   - long: "Temperature (°C)"     short: temperature   - long: "Sample concentration (µM)"     short: concentration

title: Writing data guides
text: using guide scripts, validate CUE schema catch syntax structure errors early: command : 1. Check guide excelguide_schema.cue 2. Abort detailed error message validation fails 3. Embed SHA256 hash (cue.verified field) validation succeeds hash acts proof guide validated; can verify later : R, can also check hash loading guide: warn hash missing abort hash invalid.
code: # From the data-raw folder where the schema is located: cd data-raw ./validate_and_sign.sh path/to/your/guide.yml ./verify_guide.sh path/to/your/guide.yml guide <- read_guide("path/to/guide.yml", verify_hash = TRUE)

title: Designing templates
text: provide link data structures programming languages spreadsheet consider following four types data structures template: keyvalue: key-value pair, key variable name value value variable. key value placed horizontally adjacent cells (columns). key, translated short name (see ) used parameter name scripts conform variable naming rules scripting language used. key found left-cell cell range. value can single value (one cell) vector values (multiple cells). cells: occasionally may convenient read values single cells provide keys (names) corresponding variables data guide. data stored key-value pairs, contrast keyvalue data type variable name provided template data guide must provide variable name. table: tabular data columns represent variables rows represent items variables assessed. Column names written first row used variable names. platedata: data registered row-column format microplate experiment performed. first row contains variable name left-cell, followed (integer) column names. Every subsequent row contains row name (capital letters) followed values well. variable name data read script. column row names ignored. Therefore, first row column range also empty, except variable name. Plate data stored tables , apart variables provided template two additional columns added, namely row column, corresponding row column microplate. example front page template (fitc-t4 TTR assay), illustrating number ideas concepts discuss . First page template

what: A template must have a version number
title: Designing templates
text: Templates usually evolve periods usage testing. prevent misunderstandings template version unique template version number. also needed check whether data guide compatible template version. Version numbering rules. follow R-package version rules. version number structure major.minor major.minor.patch, major, minor patch integer values. version consisting major number invalid, interpreted minor version 0, .e. version “2” interpreted “2.0”. practice means format spreadsheet cell version number recorded formally text, general number. However, Exceldataguide package provide functionality interpret fields version numbers even cells template general number format. Checking compatibilty template versions guide version. use template version numbers check compatibility guide. principle guide can used multiple versions template long locations names variables indexed guide change new template versions. case , example, explanatory texts calculations data validity checks changed template. checking version compatibility assume guide compatible consecutive range template versions minimal maximal version number. design template carefully can use data guide several versions template. , long location indexed data change, can use data guide different versions template. can specify compatible version templates data guide. package check compatibility. Clearly, use versioned data templates, hence, required field template version number.

title: Designing templates
text: strongly recommend using NA() function represent missing values templates, especially calculations. Using NA() offers several benefits: Advantages: Calculations automatically propagate NA() values subsequent formulas, avoiding errors producing sensible results Missing values handled consistently transparently Challenges: Missing values Excel require special care formulas. main issue different language settings Excel use different string representations missing values (e.g., #N/English, #NV German). creates problems: reliably detect missing values using string matching like "<>#N/", even though approach often suggested online Conditional aggregation functions (SUMIF, COUNTIF, etc.) work correctly NA() values need criterion like <>#N/detect string #N/cells Solutions: Always use ISNA() function formulas detect NA() values cells =SUM(IFNA(A1:A10, 0)) — sums values, treating NA() 0 =PRODUCT(IFNA(A1:A10, 1)) — multiplies values, treating NA() 1 =AVERAGE(IFNA(A1:A10, "")) — calculates average, treating NA() “” =COUNT(IFNA(A1:A10, "")) — counts non-missing values, treating NA() “” formulas work correctly regardless Excel’s language setting handle NA() values properly.

title: Designing templates
text: Sometimes raw measurements want exclude analysis, deleting spreadsheet advisable. Flagging (rather deleting) allows others—future self—reconsider whether measurement truly “bad,” since judgment can subjective. flag bad values: Add marker symbol (typically star asterisk) value: - 1000* *1000 — marks flagged measurement Documenting flagged values: sheet, maintain table documenting flagged value : - Cell address flagged measurement - Reason flagged creates audit trail allows someone revisit decision later. Detecting flagged values calculations: can detect “starred” values Excel using type-checking functions (ISNUMBER(), ISTEXT(), etc.) convert NA(). example: =((ISNUMBER(A1)), NA(), A1) — returns NA() cell number (.e., contains starred value like 1000*) Visual indicators: Use conditional formatting highlight flagged values distinct font color cell background, making visible glance. excelDataGuide package: package provides two utility functions work flagged values: star_to_number() — Removes star marker converts value back number has_star() — Detects values flagged (contain star/asterisk). output logical vector indicating values flagged. can used create column flags next original values.

title: Designing templates
text: keyvalue format mostly used metadata parameters. keyvalue aggregated single named list called “keyvalue”. platedata format used measured data data concerning concentrations plate wells. ranges aggregated single data frame reported variables column names, including column names “row” “col”, corresponding row column names plate.

text: excelDataGuide R package streamlines reading data standardized Excel spreadsheet templates R.

title: Read Data from templated Excel Data Reports
text: Spreadsheet templates widely used laboratories standardize data recording reduce errors. However, extracting data templates R typically requires writing custom, template-specific code. tedious error-prone.

title: Read Data from templated Excel Data Reports
text: excelDataGuide package eliminates burden : Defining data guide — simple YAML file describes data located template interpreted Reading data one command — read_data() function uses guide extract data correctly automatically data guide approach also supports FAIR principles making data structure explicit machine-readable.

title: Read Data from templated Excel Data Reports
text: can install development version excelDataGuide GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("SystemsBioinformatics/excelDataGuide")

title: Read Data from templated Excel Data Reports
text: Reading data Excel template requires just two files: template data guide. output list containing data organized according guide.
code: library(excelDataGuide)  # Path to your Excel file datafile <- system.file("extdata", "example_data.xlsx", package = "excelDataGuide")  # Path to the data guide (YAML file) guidefile <- system.file("extdata", "example_guide.yml", package = "excelDataGuide")  # Read the data data <- read_data(datafile, guidefile)

title: Read Data from templated Excel Data Reports
text: detailed guidance using package: Designing templates — Best practices structuring Excel templates (version numbers, protected cells, parameter sheets, etc.). Writing data guides — Step--step instructions creating YAML guides, examples four data types (keyvalue, cells, table, platedata) complete working example.
